title: tabernaemontana chartacea
intro: Tabernaemontana chartacea, also known as the "Malabar Star Glory" or "Wild Currant", is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, and is known to have a number of health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Tabernaemontana chartacea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments. Additionally, the plant has been traditionally used to treat snake bites and as a treatment for diarrhea and dysentery.
